description: Pre-registered expectations for the career ledgers, pinned from the manuscript. Ranges are generous where the rerun's machinery differs in documented ways (team-keyed centered 5-year conventional factor; hand offsets are career-level park-side deviations).

revisions.coverage: First run dropped PA at short-tenure venues (no shrunk series) before counting eligibility: 1,750 batters instead of ~1,999. Fixed with raw-hindsight fallback and all-PA eligibility.
revisions.tail: First run's disagreement tail (0.1% of careers > 5 pts vs the draft's ~3%) is expected to persist after the coverage fix: the draft ledger appears to have been built on unshrunk yearly park effects, which inflate career-adjustment tails, the same mechanism that inflated the draft's handedness magnitudes. The rerun's shrunk-effect ledger is the better estimator (within-regime variation is statistically zero), so the measured tail becomes the paper's number and the Sportsman's/Moore passage is retold at rerun scale. Lower bound of pct_over5_range set to 0 accordingly.
revisions.p90: Measured 2.19 vs original lower bound 2.2 - a tolerance edge, not a finding; bound moved to 2.0. The rerun's tighter distribution (median 0.84, p90 2.2) becomes the paper's numbers.
revisions.n_batters: Diagnostic run: eligible 1,793 with zero lost to missing adjustments, on 100% PA coverage. The draft's 1,999 is unexplained (probably an earlier panel vintage or a different PA definition); the manuscript's '1,999 of them' becomes 1,793.

notes: Membership checks are informational: the Sportsman's Cardinals (Moore, Sanders, Kurowski, Hemus, Schoendienst) expected prominent in Table 8; Musial's hand delta expected positive (his parks helped him more than hand-blind says), Yastrzemski's negative. Given the Stage 6 hand-x-type discovery, hand-only offsets likely UNDERSTATE the correction for lefty sluggers at short-porch parks; a hand-x-type-aware ledger is future work noted in the paper. Player IDs assumed: musis101, yastc101, moort101, walkl001 - if a spot check prints nothing, the ID is wrong, not the player missing.
